task_completion.description: Verify that the agent opens or imports the specified survey CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An application window (e.g., spreadsheet, text editor, or GIS tool) showing the contents of 'colorado_foothills_survey.csv' with columns for Point ID, Easting, Northing, Elevation, and Code.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent locates the specific points requiring elevation correction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent scrolls to, highlights, or filters for Point IDs 21, 22, 23, 24, and 25 on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent subtracts 1.52 meters from the elevation of the target points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using a formula (e.g., '=cell-1.52'), using a calculator, or manually typing the newly calculated elevation values into the Elevation (Z) column for points 21-25.

task_completion.description: Verify that the elevation values for the target points have been successfully updated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Elevation column for Point IDs 21, 22, 23, 24, and 25 displays the updated numerical values (which should be exactly 1.52 less than their original values).

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent exports the complete dataset to the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog box showing the file being saved as 'corrected_points.txt' in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a GUI application to edit the data rather than terminal shortcuts.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with a spreadsheet, text editor, or surveying GUI. There should be no terminal commands (like sed, awk, or python scripts) used to bypass manual GUI interaction.

integrity.description: Verify the agent exports the entire set of survey points, not just the modified ones.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window or export preview shows the full list of survey points (including unmodified points) being saved, rather than only points 21-25.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually performs the calculation or edits the file manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of a formula, calculator usage, or manual typing of the new values, rather than instantly pasting a fully completed file or text block from outside the environment.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input file: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\colorado_foothills_survey.csv. Output file: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\corrected_points.txt. Target Point IDs to modify: 21, 22, 23, 24, and 25. Correction: Subtract exactly 1.52 meters from the Elevation (Z) column for the target points. Tolerance for corrected values is 0.02m.
